vol_description: Equal Access Ownership Data and Files

vol_name: Ambiguity Studies - Amb Pref No Info

vol_description:
4-7 year old children are presented with Powerpoint slides illustrating six scenarios. In each scenario, 3 characters behave in a typical manner, and the 4th does something different. After each scenario, participants are asked a series of questions about the behaviors and characters.
File types: m4a, m3u, mp4

Six- to seven-year-old children were interviewed over Zoom about their perceptions of eye gaze & gaze aversion. They answered a series of forced-choice questions about vignette characters whose gaze was directed towards the teacher or away while a teacher was talking. They also answered a series of open-ended questions addressing their normative beliefs about eye gaze aversion.

vol_name: Influence of dehumanizing language on children's social judgments
vol_description: Non-autistic 8- to 10-year olds listening to 2 vignettes about a member of a novel group (humanizing vs. dehumanizing language) and responding to blatant and trait-based dehumanization scales, as well as a measure of social dominance orientation.

vol_name: Influence of dehumanizing language on children's dehumanization, punishment, and policy attitudes
vol_description: Non-autistic 8- to 10-year olds listening to a vignette about members of two novel groups (described with humanizing vs. dehumanizing language, respectively) and responding to measures of blatant dehumanization, willingness to punish the novel group members, and attitudes towards policies that would influence the group members. We also included exploratory measures of essentialism and autonomy.

vol_name: Influence of dehumanizing language on children's autism dehumanization and stereotypes
vol_description: Non-autistic 8- to 10-year olds listening to two vignettes about members of two novel groups (one autistic group & one control group, with the language used to introduce the autistic group varied between participants) and responding to measures of blatant dehumanization, warmth, competence, and expectations for behavioral tendencies towards members of the novel groups.
Video recordings and participant info from a study with 6-8 year olds
Video recordings from sessions with 6-8 year old children. We showed clips from Daniel Tiger and asked questions about children's disability beliefs and attitudes afterwards.
